question: What is the structure of a `charmcraft.yaml` file for a Kubernetes charm? Show a complete example with all required fields.

claude_output.result:
Here's the structure of a `charmcraft.yaml` for a Kubernetes charm, with a complete example:

## Required fields

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `type` | Must be `charm` |
| `name` | Charm identifier (lowercase, hyphens, numbers) |
| `summary` | One-line description (max 78 chars) |
| `description` | Longer description for Charmhub |
| `base` | OS base (e.g. `ubuntu@24.04`) |
| `containers` | Required for K8s charms — defines workload containers |

## Complete example

```yaml
name: my-app
type: charm
title: My Application
summary: A Kubernetes charm for My Application.
description: |
  This charm deploys and manages My Application on Kubernetes
  using Juju. It provides integration with common infrastructure
  charms such as ingress and PostgreSQL.

base: ubuntu@24.04

charm-user: non-root

assumes:
  - k8s-api
  - juju >= 3.6

containers:
  my-app:
    resource: my-app-image

resources:
  my-app-image:
    type: oci-image
    description: OCI image for My Application
    upstream-source: ghcr.io/example/my-app:latest

provides:
  grafana-dashboard:
    interface: grafana_dashboard
    optional: true
  metrics-endpoint:
    interface: prometheus_scrape
    optional: true

requires:
  database:
    interface: postgresql_client
    optional: true
  ingress:
    interface: ingress
    optional: true
  logging:
    interface: loki_push_api
    optional: true

peers:
  replicas:
    interface: my_app_replica
    optional: true

config:
  options:
    log-level:
      type: string
      default: info
      description: >
        The log level for the application.
        Acceptable values are debug, info, warning, error.
    port:
      type: int
      default: 8080
      description: The port the application listens on.

storage:
  data:
    type: filesystem
    location: /data
    minimum-size: 1G

charm-libs:
  - lib: traefik_k8s.ingress
    version: "2"
  - lib: data_platform_libs.data_interfaces
    version: "0"

links:
  documentation: https://discourse.charmhub.io/t/my-app-docs
  issues: https://github.com/example/my-app-operator/issues
  source: https://github.com/example/my-app-operator
  contact: https://launchpad.net/~example

analysis:
  ignore:
    attributes:
      - check-id-1
```

### Key points

- **`containers`** must reference a resource, and that resource must appear in **`resources`** with `type: oci-image`.
- **`assumes`** lets Juju check compatibility before deployment (e.g. `k8s-api` ensures a K8s model).
- **`optional: true`** on relations is a best practice from CLAUDE.md — always include it.
- **`charm-user: non-root`** enables rootless execution (requires Juju 3.6+).
- **`base`** replaces the older `bases` key — use the single-base format with modern charmcraft.
